message:
feat(cli): add --refresh/--catch-up flag to tui and search

Closes #198.

Today, launching the TUI or running cass search reflects only the index
state as of the last manual cass index run. Threads created since then
are silently invisible to search — cass knows it is stale (cass health
--json reports state.index.stale: true) but does not surface that or
offer to fix it at the point the user is about to read data that
depends on it.

Fix: add a --refresh flag (alias --catch-up) to both the tui and search
subcommands. When set, cass performs an incremental cass index pass
synchronously before the main action.

Behavior:
- Runs indexer::run_index with full=false, force_rebuild=false,
  watch=false, semantic=false — the minimum work needed to catch up.
- Indexing errors are logged (tracing::warn + stderr) but non-fatal —
  the tui/search continues against the existing (possibly stale) index
  rather than blocking the user. Matches the design called out in
  the issue: "fall through to the main action on the stale index
  rather than blocking the user".
- Emits a single "Refreshing index..." line on stderr so users know
  why there's a small delay before the TUI opens or the search runs.

Usage:
  cass tui --refresh
  cass search "oauth" --refresh
  cass tui --catch-up          # alias

No regression risk: opt-in, default off, existing invocations
unchanged. No new dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
